Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 16:08 - Thanks for your patience today, we're happy to say our staff have now restored power to all but one property in the Izane Road area. I'm sorry for any disruption we caused by having to turn off your power to repair an unexpected underground network fault.

We'd still like to keep you informed if your electricity hasn't been restored. Please call us free any time on 08003163105 if we haven't already reached out to you by phone or in person at your property.

We apologise for the outage and understand that being without power can be challenging. Rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep your lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Bev at 15:12 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Izane Road area for safety so they can repair a fault on our underground cabling network. Our estimated time to have your power back on is between 16:00 and 17:00, however, this may need to change later depending on how our works progress. If you'd like more information about why we sometimes have to switch power off to work; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/Isol. We'll keep you updated with progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 02:13 - Hello again. Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut, I know the lights being off is a worry.

Our staff have advised that further equipment and resources are required to order to complete the repairs to the generator. Due to the delay this has caused, they've advised a new time of 04:30-05:30 to have your power back on.

I know another delay is frustrating; our engineers always try to give us the best estimate that they can but due to the safety elements of working with live electricity, sometimes they have to adjust their repairs at short notice.

I'm sorry if this has caused you to change any plans you've made, but we'll be here to update you until the lights are safely back on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 01:18 - Our engineers have just called in to advise us that whilst repairs are ongoing to the generator, this is taking longer than expected.

This means it's going to take us past our current time so we are now working towards between 02:00 and 03:00 to have your power back on.

We know how disruptive being without power can be, we are working as fast as we can. If you're worried about anyone who may be more vulnerable in your property who is affected by todays outage, please don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Damask Close and Marlborough Close area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Sent by Talie at 23:47.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 22:18 - Our staff are on the way to the Damask Close area, and our estimated time frame to have power back on is currently between 02:00 and 03:00. However, this could change once our staff have assessed the local network.

We believe the power cuts you have been experiencing are caused by a fault with one of the cables. Developing faults can start very small, and so can be hard to detect but each power cut usually makes it easier to see, which means our specialist staff can detect it, and work on the repair to prevent further outages.

I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected due to a fire which affected both overhead and underground equipment that affected several thousand properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Roland at 15:36 - Our engineers have been able to divert electricity around the faulty part of the network, restoring power to 11 customers. To stop SMS updates, visit: https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP. If your power is still affected, our engineers have advised that, due to a fire in the area affecting both overhead and underground equipment, repairs are taking longer than expected. Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Roland at 14:22 - Thank you for your patience while our engineers have diverted power around the  damaged cable caused by another company working in the area in the IP29 & IP30 area, we've now restored power to most of the affected properties. There are now only 2 properties without power, who we'll speak to directly. If you're without power and haven't been contacted, then we'll need to hear from you. Please call us on 08003163105 (free from landlines and mobiles).

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the unexpected outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Sent by Theo at 13:39 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is a damaged underground cable, that initially interrupted power to 1997 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 238 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 15:30-16:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.

Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Ebony and I am the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks in Ipswich. Today's power cut was unexpected, so we were unable to provide you with prior notice to help you plan ahead. I am pleased to confirm that we managed to resolve this by diverting power to get your lights back on as soon as possible. Just so you know, we text customers when we are made aware of problems like this, so if you would like to be notified in the future you can do so here https://UKPN.short.gy/SMS This was a widespread problem on the underground network and I personally apologise for any disruption this has caused to your day. If you have any questions or are still without power, please call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut today. Our staff have confirmed they have managed to restore power to the Worthing BN11 area by diverting the power around the fault.

We believed the power cut was caused by our overhead network, however after investigations made by our field staff and our control room, we've discovered the issue was due to a fault with a piece of equipment on our underground part of the network, so we're sorry for the confusion.

I know an unexpected power cut can be very disruptive, so we apologise for the interruption. If your power isn't on, or if there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
